phrase.text: Chemical <litter> and contamination can be traced from the <tropics> to the poles and from coastlines to <abyssal> depths.
Many chemicals have <physical properties> which complicate <containment> and management efforts in the case of incidents.
Some chemicals will <dissolve> in water, while highly <volatile> chemicals will <evaporate> rapidly over a limited area.
Highly volatile chemicals may produce a fire hazard extending beyond the area <covered by> the <pool> of spilled material.
Dangerous conditions may <prevail> downwind from such a <spill> over a considerable distance.

These different physical characteristics increase the risk of high costs <arising from> the environmental impacts of the spill, such as loss of <amenities>, closure of <fisheries> and tourism activities.
Some of these chemical substances are <carcinogenic> or highly <flammable>.
<Exposure> to toxic substances is a hazard to personnel conducting <salvage> and <response> operations for incidents involving hazardous and noxious substances.
